textPlain: This is amazing -- thank you for building this! Amusingly, I too ended up searching for British computers -- there's a good article here on the Cantabrian explosion here. https://byte.tsundoku.io/#198301-042
I only have a few issues that I bought as a kid. I've been re-reading them lately and I noticed that that while e.g. a 1987 issue is deeply intellectually stimulating, a 1989 issue is kind of boring.
Wow, that’s the first digital Microfiche implementation I’ve seen. Well done!
This is the sort of thing that would be perfect to view from within Eagle Mode (https://eaglemode.sourceforge.net/).
BYTE is awesome. And this project is awesome and really well done.  I miss the BYTE days. Is there a modern day equivalent?
So much of my childhood in one zoomable image. This is incredible.

I remember having just a couple of issues of Byte but reading them cover to cover and basing my whole understanding of possibilities on a deep dive into a Silicon Graphics workstation they had an article on… happy fuzzy distant memories :)
dezoomify-rs https://byte.tsundoku.io/byte_files/10/0_0.jpgFound the following zoom levels:0. byte (Deep Zoom Image) (868480 x 453747 pixels, 376956 tiles)...I think, I'll skip downloading this

Oh wow, this is absolutely amazing. It's one thing to read about computers like the Altair in history overviews, but to see ads for it and how they were discussed at the time, that's really interesting.Connects well to the Halt and Catch Fire syllabus that was posted yesterday :) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45007414
Love this! Part of why I went into electronics is reading archives of Byte and Popular Electronics

The German 'iX magazine fuer professionelle IT' is pretty good, but it also has devops, the whole stack from networking, data storage, compute, cloud, development en ops. I bought one on holiday. It's nice to browse through pages for a change.
https://www.heise.de/select/ixI think it's only in German, but perhaps the AI can auto-translate the pdf's.
